nativeRange.habitat: Shallow to moderate-depth reef habitats; prefers lower-light zones such as reef slopes and shaded areas; tolerates turbid conditions better than most SPS corals

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Occasional supplemental feeding with coral foods (e.g., Reef Chili, amino acids, finely-ground pellet foods) has been reported to support coloration and growth. Target feeding is not considered necessary; the coral generally meets most of its energy requirements through light alone.

goodWith: other SPS corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: corals placed in direct contact (contact aggression will occur)
aggressive LPS corals with extended sweeper tentacles

summary: Astreopora is a rare-in-trade encrusting SPS coral (family Acroporidae) prized for vivid colors and low-light tolerance. Intermediate care; thrives with moderate flow and stable reef water chemistry.
careNotes:
Astreopora is a genus of approximately 17 recognized species in the family Acroporidae — the same family as Acropora and Montipora — despite its superficially massive/encrusting appearance. It is generally considered uncommon in the aquarium trade compared to other SPS genera.

Key care considerations:

• Lighting: Astreopora generally tolerates lower light levels than most SPS corals. Sources consistently recommend medium intensity (roughly 100–250 PAR). Specimens placed under very intense lighting tend to brown out. Gradual acclimation to new light conditions is advised to prevent bleaching stress.

• Flow: Moderate, indirect water movement is generally recommended. Sufficient flow helps prevent detritus from settling on the surface (which can smother polyps) and encourages polyp extension, but direct laminar flow should be avoided as it can inhibit polyp opening.

• Placement: As an encrusting coral, Astreopora can be positioned on exposed rockwork surfaces at various heights. Lower to mid rockwork placement is often preferred given its relative tolerance of reduced light. It will encrust and spread over available substrate over time.

• Aggression: Astreopora does not produce sweeper tentacles, but will engage in contact aggression with any coral it grows into directly. Adequate spacing from neighbors is recommended to prevent encrustation battles.

• Water chemistry: Like all stony corals, Astreopora consumes calcium and alkalinity as it grows. Stable alkalinity (7–9 dKH) and calcium (380–450 ppm) levels are important; supplementation via two-part dosing or a calcium reactor is commonly advised for established colonies.

• Pests: Polychaete worms have been documented colonizing the porous skeleton, which can structurally weaken colonies over time. Preventive coral dipping of new frags is recommended.

• Color: Wild colonies are reported in yellow, brown, green, pink, blue, and whitish-blue. Captive aquaculture morphs include vivid neon green, purple, and multi-color forms. Seasonal color variation has been reported under natural lighting conditions.

• Growth: Encrusting growth form; moderate growth rate. In the wild, colonies can reach up to 2 meters across (though 25–50 cm is more typical). In home aquaria, growth rate and final size are constrained by tank dimensions and husbandry conditions.

• Mature tank requirement: A well-established reef system (generally 6+ months old with stable parameters) is recommended before adding Astreopora. Parameter swings common in new tanks are more stressful for SPS corals.

• Fragging: Astreopora is considered fairly easy to frag using standard coral cutters; frags encrust and establish readily on frag plugs or rubble.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is primarily by fragging (cutting), which is straightforward: frags are cut with coral cutters and glued to frag plugs or rubble, where they encrust and grow. Sexual reproduction occurs via broadcast spawning on natural reefs but is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: Bulk Reef Supply – WWC Neon Astreopora
primary.notes: Primary source for water parameters, PAR range, flow, placement, care level, temperament, and feeding notes. WWC-branded listing cross-verified against Bullfrog Astreopora listing on same site.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply – WWC Bullfrog Astreopora
additional.notes: Cross-verified all water parameters (temp 75–78°F, SG 1.024–1.026, pH 8.1–8.4, alk 8–9 dKH, Ca 380–450, Mg 1300–1450) and PAR 150–250. Parameters matched the Neon listing exactly.

additional.name: Frag Box Corals – Astreopora Coral
additional.notes: Confirmed moderate flow, low-to-medium lighting preference, encrusting growth pattern, peaceful temperament, and photosynthetic feeding. Described lower-third placement preference consistent with reduced light tolerance.

additional.name: Reef Builders – Astreopora and Cyphastrea
additional.notes: Confirmed family Acroporidae taxonomy, encrusting growth form, moderate flow preference, low-light tolerance (performs well in lowest-light systems). Noted seasonal color variation. Cross-species comparison provides useful identification notes.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Astreopora
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Order Scleractinia, Family Acroporidae), native range (Red Sea, Indian Ocean, western Pacific), colony morphology, and polychaete pest documentation. Colony size up to 2 m diameter in wild. Not used for husbandry parameters.
